rulings.Case Title: FONGKOK ZHOU ET AL VS. MERCY HOUSING CALIFORNIA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR LEAVE TO To Add New Causes Of Action To The Second Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for February 17, 2022 line 1. PLAINTIFF FONGKOK ZHOU, SHAO ZHOU, JACK ZHOU MOTION FOR LEAVE TO To Add New Causes Of Action To The Second Amended Complaint is GRANTED. The Court will consider all newly added causes of action in the Second Amended Complaint in its ruling on the demurrer. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: FONGKOK ZHOU ET AL VS. MERCY HOUSING CALIFORNIA ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for February 17, 2022 line 1. DEFENDANT MERCY HOUSING CALIFORNIA, LUCINDA PHILLIPS, RUBY ZUNIGA, MERCY HOUSING CALIFORNIA XLIV, DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED in part and overruled in part. Demurrer is sustained as to causes of action for Housing Discrimination Based on Disability (Govt Code 12955 (a); Housing Discrimination Based on Retaliation (Govt Code 12955 (k); Failure to Reasonably Accommodate (Govt Code 12927(c)(1); UCL Violation. These claims are bared as they were either plead of could have been plead in the prior litigation and the parties admitted and affirmed that they have been fully litigated and determined in that action. Demurrer to the causes of action for Negligent and Intentional Infliction of Emotional Distress is OVERRULED. These causes of action are sufficiently stated. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Plaintiffs Second Amended Complaint

rulings.Case Title: YUNG JOO KWON ET AL VS. STEVE KIM AND ANADELIA B. KIM, INDIVIDUALLY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Notice Of Motion And Anti-Slapp Special Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiffs Complaint [Code Of Civil Procedure 425.16]
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for February 17, 2022 line 3. DEFENDANT STEVE KIM, ANADELIA KIM Defendants Notice Of Motion And Anti-Slapp Special Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiffs Complaint [Code Of Civil Procedure 425.16] is GRANTED. Moving party sustained its burden under the the first prong to show that all claims arising out of the filing of the filing of small claims actions (in purported violation of the settlement agreement) are protected under CCP 425.16. Plaintiff failed to sustain their burden and the second prong by showing probability of prevailing on the merits and litigation privilege is not adequately addressed. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: STEVENSON LOFTS SF LLC VS. KIERAN VO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff'S Motion To Enter Judgment Against Defendant For Her Failure To Comply With The Terms Of The Stipulation Agreement
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for February 17, 2022 line 4. PLAINTIFF STEVENSON LOFTS SF LLC Plaintiff'S Motion To Enter Judgment Against Defendant For Her Failure To Comply With The Terms Of The Stipulation Agreement is DENIED. No competent admissible evidence of the alleged breach provided. Furthermore, moving party failed to comply with SFRL 2.7B. Failure to comply with result in the motion being taken off calendar in the future. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303).
